commit.subject: LU-20050 sptlrpc: fix positive errno in sptlrpc_sepol_get()
commit.message:
LU-20050 sptlrpc: fix positive errno in sptlrpc_sepol_get()

sptlrpc_sepol_get() wraps the l_getsepol return code directly with
ERR_PTR(rc). On RHEL 10.1, l_getsepol fails with rc=25 (positive
ENOTTY). IS_ERR() only detects values in [-4095, -1], so the positive
value passes undetected and the caller in
ptlrpc_connect_import_locked() dereferences a garbage pointer,
causing a NULL dereference and kernel panic.

Fix by negating positive error codes before passing to ERR_PTR():

  RETURN(ERR_PTR(rc > 0 ? -rc : rc));

Signed-off-by: Hiroshi Nishida <hnishida@thelustrecollective.com>
Change-Id: If9312fce842fca133de25c4898cafd949ace6aa6
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64944
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@thelustrecollective.com>
Reviewed-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
